title: 修复发布 workflow 的精确 SHA checkout 认证

sources.reason: The exact-SHA checkout step failed to authenticate in the actual release workflow run.
Workflow regression coverage for authentication setup and exact-SHA behavior.
Required Work Item and verification workflow.

riskAssessment.reason: Release publication is blocked by authenticated exact-SHA checkout failing before tag validation; the fix must preserve source identity checks.

executionDecision.reason: The observed authentication failure, narrow workflow scope, and exact-SHA acceptance are explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Review that GitHub CLI authentication is configured before fetch, the source SHA and tag preconditions remain unchanged, and no release is published from the PR.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The release workflow authenticates its Git fetch through the configured GitHub token before fetching the exact source commit.
The workflow still requires SOURCE_COMMIT to equal GITHUB_SHA, checks the immutable tag precondition, and requires successful smoke and compatibility runs.
Workflow regression tests cover the authentication setup and the repository quality checks pass.
No release tag or GitHub Release is created by this Work Item.
guidelines: Keep exact-SHA identity checks and tag preconditions unchanged.
Use the GitHub CLI credential setup rather than embedding credentials or weakening repository permissions.
Keep publication as a separate explicit workflow action.

intent.problem: The first post-merge release dispatch failed at git fetch because the workflow's http.extraheader authentication did not authenticate the exact source checkout.
constraints: Only modify release workflow authentication and its regression test.
Do not weaken exact SHA, tag, or Actions evidence validation.
Do not publish a release in this fix Work Item.
intent.rationale: Configuring the GitHub CLI credential helper makes the existing exact-SHA checkout reliable while preserving the release gate semantics.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: release workflow configures Git authentication before fetching the exact source SHA
exact source SHA, tag uniqueness, and Actions evidence gates remain unchanged
workflow regression and project quality checks pass

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly accepted the release workflow adjustment after the observed authenticated checkout failure.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
